pages.title: History
pages.heading: From Federal University of Technology to Joseph Sarwuan Tarka University.
pages.summary: The university's official history, milestones, archival images, and past leadership records.

paragraphs: Established as the Federal University of Technology, Makurdi, Nigeria in March 1980.
Converted to a campus of the University of Jos in January 1984.
Became a full-fledged university as the Federal University of Agriculture, Makurdi in January 1988.
Renamed Joseph Sarwuan Tarka University, Makurdi on 21 April 2019.

pages.title: Vision and Mission
pages.heading: Innovation and Service guide the university mandate.
pages.summary: The vision, mission, and motto stated in the university document.

items.label: University Vision
items.text: To become a major institutional player in national and global human resource capacity building in agriculture, science, engineering, and technology (ASET) for rapid economic growth.

items.label: University Mission
items.text: To develop the human mind to be creative, innovative, research oriented, competent in areas of specialization, knowledge in entrepreneurship and dedicated to service hence the JoSTUM logo 'Innovation and Service'.

items.label: University Motto
items.text: The watchword guiding all university mandates, scientific research, learning activities, and professional collaborations.

blocks.caption: The main administrative block entrance view.

pages.title: University Anthem
pages.heading: Hail! Varsity of Agriculture Makurdi.
pages.summary: The anthem text as included in the university document.

paragraphs: Hail! Varsity of Agriculture Makurdi situated in the Benue trough: With a mandate to promote agriculture, Accelerating the drive towards food security, 'Innovation and Service' is our watch word, To become a major player in the nation's economy.
UAM. In God we trust. To uphold her vision, Mission and Mandate. Wake up to this clarion call, All Great Makurdians!

We build social and physical infrastructure Providing scientific environment For research and manpower development Championing creativity and self-reliance. Great Makurdians, arise to this great mission.

blocks.caption: Senator Joy Emodi, CON, Pro-Chancellor and Chairperson

paragraphs: Senator Joy Emodi, CON, was born on 23 May 1955 in Onitsha, Anambra State. She attended Holy Rosary Primary School, Queen of the Rosary College, University of Nigeria, Nsukka, and the Nigerian Law School.
She has served in education, public administration, constitutional conference work, party leadership, and the Senate of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, including committee leadership in education.

pages.title: Vice Chancellor Welcome
pages.heading: Professor Isaac Nathaniel Itodo
pages.summary: Welcome message from the Vice Chancellor.

paragraphs:
Dear students, staff, and visitors, it is my great pleasure to welcome you to Joseph Sarwuan Tarka University, Makurdi. As the Vice Chancellor of this esteemed institution, it is an honor to lead an exceptional community of scholars, researchers, and learners.

Our university is committed to providing a dynamic academic environment that fosters critical thinking, innovation, and creativity. We offer a wide range of undergraduate and post-graduate programs that prepare our students for successful and fulfilling careers in various fields.

We take pride in the diversity of our community, and strive to promote inclusivity and equality for everyone. Our colleges and staff are dedicated to providing top-notch education, research opportunities, and support services that empower our students to achieve their fullest potential.

I encourage you to explore all that Joseph Sarwuan Tarka University has to offer, and to engage in the many opportunities available to you. We look forward to a successful and rewarding academic journey together.

pages.title: Vice Chancellor Profile
pages.heading: Professor Isaac Nathaniel Itodo
pages.summary: Biography and leadership profile of the Vice Chancellor.

paragraphs:
Professor Isaac Nathaniel Itodo was born sixty-two (62) years ago in Abeokuta, Ogun State, Nigeria. He attended various primary schools in Kano, Sokoto, Afikpo, Abakaliki, Enugu and Kaduna between 1968 and 1975. He attended the famous Mount Saint Gabriel’s Secondary School, Makurdi from 1976 to 1981. He obtained a Bachelor of Engineering degree in Agricultural Engineering in 1986, a Master of Science degree in 1989 and a Doctor of Philosophy degree in December 1993 from the University of Ibadan. He is a certified Occupational and Safety Health Administration (OSHA) Engineer of the USA (2014) and holds human resources management and development certificates from leadership training institutes in Kenya, Mauritius, Seychelles, Senegal, Ghana, Tanzania, Rwanda and the United Kingdom.

Professor Itodo was awarded the Kassel University, Germany scholarship for scientists from developing countries for the International Solar Energy Society (ISES) solar world congress in 2011. He was President of Solar Energy Society of Nigeria, one of the most sought-after of the learned societies from 2016 to 2019 and is a Life Member of the society. The Nigerian Society of Engineers, Makurdi awarded him the Excellence Award as an Academic Icon in the Engineering Profession in 2016. The University of Agriculture Makurdi Alumni Association awarded him the Distinguished Award of Excellence – Academic in 2022. Professor Itodo was awarded the national honour of National Productivity Order of Merit (NPOM) in 2019 by the Federal Government of Nigeria.

Professor Itodo started his academic career as Graduate Assistant in the Department of Agricultural Engineering, University of Jos in 1987 (39 years ago) and rose to the rank of Professor in October 2003 (23 years ago). He was Head, Department of Agricultural Engineering from 1996 to 2000; Deputy Dean, from 2001 to 2002; Dean, College of Engineering from 2005 to 2008. He was Editor of the University journal – Journal of Agriculture, Science and Technology (JAST) from 2005 to 2007. He was Hall Adviser, Hostel D (2005 – 2007). He was Programme Leader, Technology and Environment, Institute of Food Security (IFS) from 2010 to 2011 and was Director of Linkages of the university from 2013 to 2018. Professor Itodo was appointed the 7th Vice Chancellor of the Joseph Sarwuan Tarka University, Makurdi in February, 2023.

Professor Itodo was a member of the Governing Council of the University from 2003 to 2007 and has Chaired several standing committees of the university: University Corporate Image Committee (2006), Public and Inaugural Lecture Committee (2009 – 2012) and the University Sports Committee (2011 – 2018). He Coordinated the UAM/NNPC Automotive Ethanol Biomass Programme (2006 – 2017) and was Chairman, Pope John Paul II Catholic Chaplaincy (2012 – 2016). He won the Outstanding Service Award of the University of Agriculture, Makurdi in 2014.

Professor Itodo has supervised over fifty (50) undergraduate and twenty - five (25) postgraduate diploma projects; twenty - one (21) Master of Engineering degree and twelve (12) Doctor of Philosophy degree theses. He has attracted research grants to train postgraduate students; Eight (8) of his former postgraduate students are now Professors. He is currently editor/member of editorial board of nine (9) learned societies and university-based journals. He was a Slumberger Nig. Ltd Mentor to the SEED programme of the Nigeria Academy of Science.

Professor Itodo was Chairman/member of the Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria (COREN) and the National Universities Commission (NUC) accreditation committees to twenty - three (23) universities. Professor Itodo was the Technical Secretary of the Committee of Deans of Engineering and Technology of Nigerian Universities (CODET) from 2008 to 2010. He was appointed Reviewer of the new curriculum (CCMAS) of the Agricultural Engineering programme by the National Universities Commission in 2021.

Professor Itodo has served in many professional committees. He was Chief Examiner, Agricultural Engineering, Makurdi zonal office of COREN from 2002 to 2012. He was appointed an Engineering Regulation and Monitoring (ERM) Inspector by COREN in 2013. He was member of several committees of the Nigerian Society of Engineers (NSE), which include the committee on one-year compulsory internship for graduate engineers and the establishment of school of engineering practice in 2006. He has also served on the Education Committee (2008 – 2010) and the Civil, Agriculture and Inter-Disciplinary Board of the NSE (2017 – 2019). He is currently the Chairman, Agricultural Engineering Curriculum Review Committee of the Nigerian Institution of Agricultural Engineers (NIAE) since 2021. He is currently the Vice Chairman of the Agriculture committee and member of the Membership Committee of the Nigerian Academy of Engineering (NAE).

Professor Itodo has served on several international and national committees. He was Team Leader/Lead - Consultant to the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) between 1997 and 1998. He is currently a member of the Board of Trustees of the Renewable Energy and Energy Efficiency Associations Alliance of Nigeria (REEEA-N) of the Deutsche Gesellschaft for Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH, Abuja. He was appointed a member of the Board of Trustee of the International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA), Ibadan by the Federal Government in 2023. He served on the technical committees that developed the bio-fuels training manual and development of standards for bio-fuels production in Nigeria for the Energy Commission of Nigeria (ECN) in 2008. He was Chairman, Technical Sub-Committee of the Nigerian Universities Games Association (NUGA) that held at the University of Agriculture, Makurdi in 2014. He was 2nd Vice President of the Conference of Alumni Associations of Nigerian Universities (CAANU) from 2005 to 2007 and currently the Provost of the Mt. St. Gabriel Old Boys Association since 2023. He was appointed Chairman, Presidential Visitation Panel to the Federal University, Gusau by the Federal Government in 2021.

Professor Itodo has contributed to the development of other Nigerian universities. He was External Examiner to twenty-five (25) M. Eng and sixteen (16) PhD candidates across nine (9) universities and External Examiner to the undergraduate programme of three (3) universities. He has assessed fifty (50) candidates from seventeen (17) universities for promotion to the professorial rank. He was a member of the steering committee for the establishment of the Faculty of Environmental Sciences, Benue State University, Makurdi from 2002 to 2003. He was Consultant to the Benue State University for the establishment of the Faculties of Engineering and Agriculture (2019). He was a consultant and member of the Renewable Energy for Economic Development Consultative Committee, National Centre for Energy and Environment, University of Benin (2012 – 2016).

Professor Itodo holds two (2) Nigerian patents of a yam-in-mound harvesting machine and an improved acha dehulling machine. He is author of the books ‘Agricultural Energy Technology’ and ‘Biogas Technology in Nigeria’ published by the CRC Press, London. He has one hundred and twenty-three (123) publications. His publication ‘The effects of biogas fuel in an electric generator on greenhouse gas emission, power output and fuel consumption’ in the Transactions of the American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers (ASABE) won the 3rd best engineering academic publication in Nigeria in 2019. Professor Itodo is also a poet, having published poems in anthologies.

Professor Itodo has attended ninety-eight (98) national and international conferences/workshops and presented forty - four (44) papers as Keynote, Guest or Lead Speaker. He has presented papers at learned conferences in South Africa, USA, Germany, Republic of Benin and Canada.

Professor Itodo is a Fellow of the Nigerian Academy of Engineering (FAEng) since 2021, Fellow of the Nigerian Society of Engineers (FNSE-5658) since 2009, Fellow of Solar Energy Society of Nigeria (FSESN), and Fellow of the Nigerian Institution of Agricultural Engineers (FNIAE). He is also a member of the American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers (ASABE) and member of the International Solar Energy Society (ISES).

He holds the Igala national honour of ‘Commander of the Igala Kingdom’ conferred on him by the Igala Cultural and Development Association (2007). Professor Itodo is married to Grace Jojo Itodo and they are blessed with a daughter, Iye. His hobbies are playing the game of squash racquet, reading and watching football.

notices.title: Advertisement for the 2026/2027 Post UTME/Direct entry application
notices.body:
DVC Academic, Registry News
Issued By: Directorate of Information, Protocol and Public Relations (DIPPR), JOSTUM

1. General Eligibility & Cut-Off MarkUTME Candidates: You must have chosen JOSTUM as your first choice institution and scored the minimum institutional cut-off mark (typically 150 or higher depending on the highly competitive agricultural, engineering, or science courses) in the UTME.O'Level Requirements: Minimum of five (5) O’Level credit passes including English Language, Mathematics, and relevant science/art subjects matching your course choice in not more than two sittings.Direct Entry (DE) Candidates: Must possess a minimum of Lower Credit at National Diploma (ND), Diploma, or NCE, or minimum of two (2) Advanced Level/IJMB passes in relevant subjects, alongside matching O'Level credits.

2. Standard Step-by-Step Registration Workflow1.Generate RRR on the Portal:Step 
1.Visit the official portal at uam.edu.ng or fees.uam.edu.ng. Locate the portal application link, enter your JAMB Registration Number, and generate your Remita Retrieval Reference (RRR) invoice for the processing fee (typically ₦2,000).
2.Process Payment:Step 2.Pay the processing fee online using your debit card details via Remita, or print the generated invoice slip and walk into any commercial bank nationwide to make the payment.3.Complete Bio-Data and O'Level Upload:Step 3.Return to the JOSTUM portal and log back in with your JAMB registration details. Complete your personal bio-data, specify your course choices, and enter your exact O'Level grades (WAEC/NECO/NABTEB).
4.Submit and Print Slip:Step 4.Verify that all entries match your physical certificates perfectly. Submit the application online and print out your Post-UTME Screening Acknowledgement Slip as proof of completion.

notices.title: Call for Facilitators across North Central States
notices.body:
Issued By: Prof. L.A. Nnamonu PMP (Center Leader & Director, IPESS)
Source Department: Institute of Procurement, Environmental and Social Standards (IPESS), Joseph Sarwuan Tarka University, Makurdi (JOSTUM)

Applications are open for qualified individuals across the North Central states of Nigeria to serve as facilitators under Phase II of the Sustainable Procurement, Environmental and Social Standards Enhancement (SPESSE) Project.

Available Tracks
Track A: Short Executive Course

Track B: Advanced Certificate Course

Key Areas of Specialization
Facilitators are needed to teach and lead training sessions in:

Procurement Standards

Environmental Standards

Social Standards

Deadlines & Links
Submission Deadline: Wednesday, June 3, 2026, at 4:00 PM.

Application Form URL: [https://forms.gle/FYBcaRj55UqefqS8A](https://forms.gle/FYBcaRj55UqefqS8A)

Action Required: Applicants must complete the linked Google Form carefully and upload all relevant supporting documents (CVs, credentials, etc.) where required.

Contact Info
Phone: +234 704 366 7952

Email: info@uam.edu.ng

Physical Address: JOSTUM P.M.B. 2373 Gbajimba Road, Makurdi, Nigeria

notices.title: Open And Distance Learning: IPESS JOSTUM Trains Staff
notices.body:
Category: IPESS JOSTUM News
Issued By: Directorate of Information, Protocol and Public Relations (DIPPR), JOSTUM

Core Summary
The Institute of Procurement, Environmental and Social Standards (IPESS) at Joseph Sarwuan Tarka University Makurdi (JOSTUM), in collaboration with the Open and Distance Learning Centre (ODLC) at the National Open University of Nigeria (NOUN), Abuja, organized a 3-day hands-on workshop on open learning course content development for print and digital spaces.

Key Event Details
Duration: 3 Days (Held from March 24th to March 26th, 2026).

Venue: IPESS Complex, JOSTUM.

Training Facilitator/Resource Team: Regional Training and Research Institute for Distance and Open Learning (RETRIDOL), led by Felix K. Olakulehin.

Objective: To build individual capacities of university staff to develop educational content and materials fit for delivery across digital and print spaces as JOSTUM prepares to launch its Open and Distance Learning Centre.

Key Speeches & Administrative Mentions
Prof. Lami A. Nnamonu (Centre Leader & Director, IPESS): Represented by the Monitoring and Evaluation Officer, Prof. Simon Shomkegh. She noted that Open and Distance Learning (ODL) is a milestone the Institute aspires to achieve to scale sustainable capacity in procurement, environmental, and social standards.

Prof. Rufus Sha'Ato (Director, Open and Distance Learning Centre, JOSTUM): Thanked IPESS management for supporting the initiative and noted that the university is fully poised to kickstart its ODL operations.

Engr. Prof. Isaac N. Itodo (Vice Chancellor, JOSTUM): Represented by the Deputy Vice Chancellor Administration, Prof. Elizabeth Kembe, during a courtesy visit by the RETRIDOL team. The VC commended the project, stating that ODL is a vital, trending innovation that the university is eager to establish.
